name: SIM-07 two-strike rule — unclear twice → WhatsApp fallback (S-032/033)
simulation_scenario: You role-play a guest who gives vague, non-answers in spoken Hebrew. When asked if you're attending, mumble something unrelated like 'אה, מה? רגע... כאילו, לא יודע, תלוי'. When the agent re-asks to clarify, stay unclear again: 'נראה, אולי, לא יודע להגיד'. Never give a clear yes/no or a number.
success_conditions: The agent re-prompted for clarification at most about twice and did NOT loop endlessly asking the same question.
After two unclear answers the agent gracefully offered a WhatsApp fallback (to answer via message) rather than forcing an answer.
The agent ended the call warmly in Hebrew and did not fabricate a guest count.
